title: teruncius arvensis
intro: Teruncius arvensis, also known as Corn marigold or Pot marigold, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant's petals and flowers have been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Teruncius arvensis include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate eye and skin irritations, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for colds, sore throats, and wounds. Herbal preparations of Teruncius arvensis can be made into teas, infusions, salves, and creams for topical application, showcasing the plant's versatility in traditional herbal medicine.
